nodes.name: getMcDataForBot
nodes.description: Returns the minecraft-data (mcData) object for the provided bot's version. Validates bot and bot.version and throws informative errors if missing. This function is self-contained and does not depend on any external helper functions.

nodes.name: reportErrorAndRethrow
nodes.description: Utility function that sends a chat message describing an error (if bot.chat is available) and then rethrows the original error. Designed to replace repetitive catch blocks that log to chat and rethrow. Self-contained and does not depend on any external helpers.

nodes.name: ensureWoodLogs
ensureCobblestone
ensureResource
nodes.description: A single, parameterized ensure function that covers the sibling ensure* skills. resourceType chooses behavior: 'cobblestone' mines stone to get cobblestone (ensures a pickaxe), 'logs' and 'wood_logs' search for and mine nearby log blocks (optionally exploring), and a generic fallback tries to mine a block named the same as resourceType. The function reports progress via bot.chat, respects targetTotal and maxAttempts, and uses exploreTime for exploration-based resource types.

nodes.name: getMcDataAndHelpers
nodes.description: Provides a single entrypoint that initializes mcData (must be declared at the top) and returns common inventory helper functions used across multiple crafting skills. The function is self-contained and defines all helper functions inside its body. Usage: const { mcData, getItemCount, findPlankVariantInInventory, ... } = await getMcDataAndHelpers(bot);

nodes.name: waitForInventorySync
nodes.description: Small self-contained helper that waits a few server ticks for inventory/behavior synchronization. Uses bot.waitForTicks if present, otherwise falls back to a timeout approximating Minecraft ticks (50ms). Parameters: bot (required), ticks (number, default 2).

craftFurnace

versions.change_log: Modified to call extracted subskill ensureBotAndPositiveCount

nodes.name: ensureBotAndPositiveCount
nodes.description: A small self-contained validator that throws if bot is missing or if a provided count parameter is not a positive number. Keeps error text compatible with existing skills by defaulting the paramName to 'count'.

nodes.name: smeltIronIngots
craftIronPickaxe
generalCraftTool
nodes.description: A single, parameterized crafting skill that produces a tool from a given material and toolType. Supports material-specific provisioning flows for 'iron' (smelt/ensure ore), 'stone' (mine cobblestone), and 'wood' (craft/use specific plank variant). It ensures sticks, a crafting table (if required), and then crafts the requested tool(s). Parameters: bot (mineflayer bot), material (string: 'iron' | 'stone' | 'wood' | ...), toolType (string, e.g. 'pickaxe'|'axe'), count (how many tools to craft), options (object: plankVariant, maxTableDistance, maxMineAttempts, smeltTimeout, ensureIronAttempts, exploreTime). Relies on helper skills/functions available in the environment: craftItem, mineBlock, smeltIronIngots, ensureIronOre, craftSticks, ensureLogs, setupCraftingTable, placeItem, findNearbyOpenArea, waitForInventorySync, etc. Provides best-effort fallbacks when some helpers are missing.

nodes.name: smeltIngots
nodes.description: Generic smelting function that handles smelting various ingot types (e.g., gold_ingot, iron_ingot). It unifies the logic for checking existing ingots, gathering raw materials (via ensureResource or mining/exploration), ensuring/placing a furnace, selecting fuel, and calling smeltItem to perform the smelting. Use the ingotType parameter (e.g., 'gold_ingot' or 'iron_ingot') to select behavior.

nodes.name: initMcDataVec3
nodes.description: Provides a self-contained initializer that loads and returns mcData (minecraft-data for the bot's version) and Vec3 from the vec3 package. Validates bot and bot.version and throws helpful errors on failure. Intended to replace repeated require(...) calls at the top of asynchronous skills.

nodes.name: provideInventoryHelpers
nodes.description: Provides a self-contained set of inventory and data helpers: mcData (from minecraft-data for the bot version), Vec3 (from vec3), and getItemCount(bot, name) to count items in the bot inventory. Callers should await this function (e.g. const { mcData, Vec3, getItemCount } = await provideInventoryHelpers(bot)).
